rationale: The citing paper uses Berger and Fitzsimons (2008) in its core framing to support that environmental cues can prime concepts and influence product evaluation and choice. It also cites this paper when arguing that such environmental information is often processed automatically and nonconsciously, which underpins the proposed mechanism. Although cited in groups, these claims are central to the conceptual setup and hypothesis logic of the article, not just a peripheral literature list mention.
relation_description: Berger and Fitzsimons (2008) functions as a conceptual foundation for the argument that incidental environmental cues can nonconsciously shape consumer judgments and choices. The citing paper builds on that premise by introducing spatial centrality as a new environmental cue linked to risk-taking.
